summary: Large fighter decomp PR that moved much of the common damage code out of `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` into a new `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Damage.c`/`.h`, added it to `obj_files.mk`, introduced names for several damage/knockback helpers and callbacks, and adjusted fighter/common headers and tooling to support WIP/nonmatching comparison. Evidence for intent beyond the title is limited because the PR body only contains an image and there were no review comments.
what_changed: Removed thousands of lines from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` and added a 4416-line `ftCo_Damage.c` implementation for common damage behavior.
Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Damage.h` with prototypes for damage angle, knockback, velocity, animation, IASA, physics, collision, fly, and fly-roll callbacks.
Added `$(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Damage.c.o` to `obj_files.mk` so the new C file participates in the text object build.
Renamed callsites from anonymous or address-style helpers to more descriptive symbols such as `ftCo_Damage_CalcAngle`, `ftCo_Damage_CalcKnockback`, and `ftCo_Damage_CalcVel`.
Added `ftCo_DamageVars` to `ftCommon_MotionVars`, mapping damage motion scratch data at `fp+2340` through `fp+235B`.

Expanded fighter type/header knowledge around damage fields such as `dmg.x1848_kb_angle`, `dmg.kb_applied`, `dmg.x1838_percentTemp`, `dmg.x184c_damaged_hurtbox`, `dmg.x18ac_time_since_hit`, and `x8c_kb_vel`.

Converted HSD pad constants in `common_structs.h` from `static const` names like `HSD_Pad_A` to preprocessor macros like `HSD_PAD_A` and `HSD_PAD_LR`, with broad callsite churn to use the macro names.
Adjusted asm-differ tooling to compare WIP builds against expected builds, including `build/wip/ssbm.us.1.2`, `expected_dir`, and `WIP=1` makeflags.
Added vim helper commands for searching fighter struct offsets in headers and labels in expected dumps.
smart_moves: Kept progress mergeable by using `#if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP)` assembly blocks alongside readable C alternatives for functions that were not yet fully matched.
Created a focused `ftCo_Damage.h` instead of continuing to expose damage functions only through broad legacy headers.
Used address comments in prototypes, for example `/* 08D7F0 */`, preserving lookup value while allowing names to become semantic.

Named the highest-value helpers around game concepts first: angle calculation, knockback scaling, and velocity merging are much easier to search than raw `ft_8008D7F0`, `ft_8008D930`, and local label names.
Preserved decomp workflow support by updating asm-differ paths and makeflags for WIP builds rather than forcing all work through matching-only builds.
Added M2CTX stack struct declarations in the header for difficult stack-shaped functions, making decompiler output easier to iterate on without pretending all stack layout is understood.
decomp_lessons: For large action-state decomp, splitting the file and landing partial matches can be practical if unmatched functions are guarded with assembly fallbacks under matching builds.
When an asm function becomes a named C helper, update existing asm and C callsites immediately so future diffs carry semantic names.

Damage code touches many systems: fighter state, motion vars, collision, background flash, input, item tables, camera/effects, and math helpers. Header hygiene and forward declarations are part of the match, not afterthoughts.
Motion-var unions should get a per-action struct even when fields are still partially named; `ftCo_DamageVars` localizes `fp+2340` scratch use and avoids raw offset spread.
Tooling changes may be necessary for partial decomp PRs: asm-differ must know how to compare a WIP object against expected objects when the normal build layout changes.
Use `MUST_MATCH`/`WIP` gates carefully: they document which code is explanatory/nonmatching while preserving exact matching behavior where required.
naming_conventions: Common fighter damage functions use the `ftCo_Damage_*` prefix for semantic helpers and action callbacks.
Action callbacks follow the standard suffix pattern `_Anim`, `_IASA`, `_Phys`, and `_Coll`, including `ftCo_Damage_Anim`, `ftCo_DamageFly_Anim`, and `ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Coll`.
Address-only functions that remain unknown keep `ft_8008....` or `ftCo_8008....` names, sometimes with address comments in headers.
Pad bit names were normalized to uppercase macro style: `HSD_PAD_A`, `HSD_PAD_LR`, `HSD_PAD_AB`, `HSD_PAD_XY`.
Partially understood damage fields combine offset and meaning, such as `x1848_kb_angle`, `kb_applied`, `kb_squat_mul`, `kb_ice_mul`, `kb_smashcharge_mul`, and `kb_min`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used inline assembly blocks with `nofralloc` inside `#if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP)` to keep exact codegen available.
Readable C implementations are compiled under WIP/nonmatching mode, enabling iterative decomp without breaking matching builds.
Preserved label/address breadcrumbs like `/* 08D7F0 */`, `lbl_8008D830`, and prototype address comments for cross-referencing with disassembly.
Replaced local-label callsites such as calls to `.L_8008DC0C` with named calls to `ftCo_Damage_CalcVel` once the helper boundary was identified.
Updated asm-differ to compute the expected object path relative to the WIP build dir, then fall back from `/src/` to `/asm/` and `.c.o` to `.s.o` when needed.
Added `expected_dir` and switched asm-differ settings to `build/wip/ssbm.us.1.2` with `WIP=1`, matching the mixed C/asm workflow.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match functions left under `#if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP)` in `ftCo_Damage.c`.
Search for subsequent renames of `ftCo_8008DA4C`, `ftCo_8008E5A4`, `ft_8008DCE0`, and other address-style damage helpers.
Check later struct-layout PRs for improved names for `ftCo_DamageVars` fields `x0`, `x4`, `x8`, `x14`, `x1A`, and `x1B`.
Look for follow-up changes to the WIP asm-differ settings introduced here.
Search for callsite migration from `ft_08A4.h` to the dedicated `ftCo_Damage.h`.
